title: Elektronisko dokumentu likums
description: Latvian law governing electronic documents, electronic signatures, and their legal equivalence with paper documents and handwritten signatures. Complements Regulation (EU) 910/2014 (eIDAS).

provisions.title: Purpose of the Law
provisions.content: The purpose of this Law is to establish the legal framework for the creation, processing, storage, and transmission of electronic documents, and to define the legal effect of electronic documents and electronic signatures. This Law complements Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 of the European Parliament and of the Council on electronic identification and trust services for electronic transactions in the internal market (eIDAS Regulation).

provisions.title: Scope
provisions.content: This Law applies to the creation, processing, storage, and transmission of electronic documents in legal transactions between natural persons, legal persons, and state and local government institutions. This Law does not apply to transactions where specific laws require a document to be in paper form or to have a notarised signature.

provisions.title: Definitions
provisions.content: For the purposes of this Law: 1) Electronic document means a document created, stored, and transmitted in electronic form, the content of which is fixed in digital data; 2) Electronic signature means data in electronic form which is attached to or logically associated with other electronic data and which is used by the signatory to sign; 3) Qualified electronic signature means an advanced electronic signature that is created by a qualified electronic signature creation device and is based on a qualified certificate, as defined in Regulation (EU) 910/2014.

provisions.title: Legal Equivalence of Electronic Documents
provisions.content: An electronic document shall not be denied legal effect and admissibility as evidence in legal proceedings solely on the grounds that it is in electronic form. An electronic document that complies with the requirements of this Law shall have the same legal effect as a paper document.

provisions.title: Legal Effect of Electronic Signatures
provisions.content: A qualified electronic signature shall have the equivalent legal effect of a handwritten signature in accordance with Article 25(2) of Regulation (EU) 910/2014. An electronic signature that does not meet the requirements for a qualified electronic signature shall not be denied legal effect solely on the grounds that it is in electronic form or that it does not meet the requirements for a qualified electronic signature.

provisions.title: Electronic Seals
provisions.content: An electronic seal shall not be denied legal effect and admissibility as evidence in legal proceedings solely on the grounds that it is in electronic form or that it does not meet the requirements for a qualified electronic seal. A qualified electronic seal shall enjoy the presumption of integrity of the data and of correctness of the origin of that data, in accordance with Article 35 of Regulation (EU) 910/2014.

provisions.title: Electronic Time Stamps
provisions.content: An electronic time stamp shall not be denied legal effect and admissibility as evidence in legal proceedings solely on the grounds that it is in electronic form or that it does not meet the requirements for a qualified electronic time stamp. A qualified electronic time stamp shall enjoy the presumption of the accuracy of the date and time it indicates and the integrity of the data to which the date and time are bound, in accordance with Article 41 of Regulation (EU) 910/2014.

provisions.chapter: Chapter III - Requirements for Electronic Documents
provisions.title: Requirements for Electronic Documents in Public Administration
provisions.content: Electronic documents submitted to and issued by state and local government institutions shall be signed with a qualified electronic signature or sealed with a qualified electronic seal. The Cabinet of Ministers shall determine the format and technical requirements for electronic documents exchanged with public administration institutions.

provisions.chapter: Chapter III - Requirements for Electronic Documents
provisions.title: Storage of Electronic Documents
provisions.content: Electronic documents shall be stored in a manner that ensures their integrity, readability, and accessibility for the entire retention period required by law. The person responsible for the storage of electronic documents shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to prevent unauthorised alteration, deletion, or loss of the documents.

provisions.chapter: Chapter III - Requirements for Electronic Documents
provisions.title: Transmission of Electronic Documents
provisions.content: Electronic documents may be transmitted by electronic mail, through state information systems, or by other electronic means that ensure the identification of the sender and the integrity of the document. Where a law or regulation requires the delivery of a document, an electronic document shall be deemed delivered when the addressee has been able to access it.

provisions.title: Trust Service Providers
provisions.content: Trust service providers operating in Latvia shall comply with the requirements of Regulation (EU) 910/2014 and this Law. Qualified trust service providers shall be subject to supervision by the designated supervisory body. The supervisory body shall maintain and publish a trusted list of qualified trust service providers and the qualified trust services provided by them.

provisions.title: Supervisory Body
provisions.content: The National Agency for Information Technologies (LVRTC — Latvijas Valsts radio un televīzijas centrs) shall serve as the supervisory body for qualified trust service providers in accordance with Article 17 of Regulation (EU) 910/2014. The supervisory body shall carry out regular audits of qualified trust service providers and take enforcement action where necessary.

provisions.title: Liability of Trust Service Providers
provisions.content: A qualified trust service provider shall be liable for damage caused intentionally or negligently to any natural or legal person due to a failure to comply with the obligations under Regulation (EU) 910/2014 and this Law. The burden of proving the absence of intentional or negligent conduct shall lie with the qualified trust service provider, in accordance with Article 13 of Regulation (EU) 910/2014.

provisions.title: National Electronic Identification Scheme
provisions.content: Latvia shall maintain a national electronic identification scheme that meets the requirements for notification under Article 9 of Regulation (EU) 910/2014. The national identity card (eID) and the electronic identification system based on it shall serve as the primary means of electronic identification for access to public services.

provisions.title: Mutual Recognition
provisions.content: Electronic identification means issued under notified electronic identification schemes of other EU Member States shall be recognised for the purpose of cross-border authentication in accessing public online services, in accordance with Article 6 of Regulation (EU) 910/2014. The level of assurance of the electronic identification means shall be equal to or higher than the level required by the national public online service.

provisions.title: Security Requirements for eID
provisions.content: The electronic identification scheme shall implement appropriate security measures to ensure the reliability, confidentiality, and integrity of the electronic identification process. These measures shall include multi-factor authentication, secure issuance procedures, cryptographic protection of identity data, and regular security assessments. The Cabinet of Ministers shall determine specific security requirements.

provisions.title: Transitional Provisions
provisions.content: Electronic documents and electronic signatures created before the entry into force of this Law shall retain their legal effect if they comply with the legal requirements that were in force at the time of their creation. Trust service providers operating at the time of entry into force of Regulation (EU) 910/2014 shall bring their operations into compliance with the requirements of the Regulation within the timeframe specified therein.

definitions.definition: A document created, stored, and transmitted in electronic form, the content of which is fixed in digital data.
Data in electronic form which is attached to or logically associated with other electronic data and which is used by the signatory to sign.
An advanced electronic signature created by a qualified electronic signature creation device and based on a qualified certificate, as defined in Regulation (EU) 910/2014.
A natural or legal person providing one or more trust services as defined in Regulation (EU) 910/2014.
